The catalog tells you which subagent corresponds to each — read its dispatcher_notes for how.
231	
232	**Make the call, then proceed — never defer the decision to the user.** When the critic (or your own analysis) surfaces a strategy fork, do NOT turn it into a blocking question. Choose the option best supported by the evidence and record it in `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` under a `## Decision made` section: the option chosen, why, the LOC/risk trade-off you weighed, and the cheapest signal that would make you reverse it. Then dispatch provers on that option THIS iter. Review surfaces it in TO_USER.md as an FYI the user can override by adding a hint to `USER_HINTS.md`; if a contrary hint appears, the next plan agent revisits — otherwise the project simply keeps moving on your choice. What you must NEVER produce: a "no prover dispatch this iter — awaiting decision" round, an options menu with a "where to reply", or a "default to X if no reply" framing. The user is not on call; a question no one answers must not stall the loop.
233	
234	**Deeper-think trigger summary.** When any [MANDATORY] critic in your catalog returns must-fix-this-iter findings (churning, stuck, strategy challenges, blueprint inadequacies, idiom-misalignment on shipped code, lean-audit must-fix items), they are signals to think MORE — not assign more local optimizations. Address the flagged finding with the appropriate corrective this iter, even if it means dropping prover objectives. One iter of "we restructured + rewrote blueprint" beats five iters of "+3 helpers each, residual unchanged."
235	
236	## Subagent delegation
237	
238	Each subagent in your catalog is one tool. The catalog includes its description, write-domain hint, MANDATORY / read-only / can-spawn flags, and (under "Workflow guidance") its `dispatcher_notes` — *that's the canonical guidance for how to use that subagent*. Read the descriptor's full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` before composing the directive.
239	
240	### How to invoke
241	
242	Pick a kebab-case **slug** (each call within an iter must use a distinct slug — e.g. `split-wlocal`, `m1b-route`). Write the directive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md`, then run via the Bash tool (foreground, one call):
243	
244	```
245	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
246	  --name <subagent-name> \
247	  --slug <slug> \
248	  --directive-file .archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md \
249	  --write-domain '<glob>' \
250	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
251	```
252	
253	The wrapper prints a one-line status and exits 0 on success. `ARCHON_ITER_NUM` is set by the loop — no need to pass `--iter-num`.
254	
255	**Treat each dispatch as blocking.** Don't deliberately pass `run_in_background: true`. The wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it returns only once the child finishes and its report is written), but a dispatch is long-running, so the harness may auto-background it and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected. Either way, await the task / poll for the report at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` before you act on the result; never continue planning as if a still-running dispatch had already returned.
256	
257	**Directives must be fully self-contained.** Subagents do not read `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state; they read what you tell them to. Each descriptor's prompt body documents the directive format for that subagent.
258	
259	**Write-domain** globs constrain what the subagent (and any descendants it spawns) may modify. Common: `'Algebra/**'`, `'Algebra/WLocal.lean'`, `'task_results/**'` for read-only subagents. Children's declared domains must be a subset of yours.
260	
261	**Parallelism**: dispatch multiple subagents concurrently by issuing multiple Bash calls in one message. The dispatch semaphore caps total concurrent processes by `loop.max_parallel`.
262	
263	### After each subagent returns
264	
265	The subagent's report lands at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (or `task_results/<parent-slug>/<name>-<slug>.md` when nested). The loop auto-archives it to `logs/iter-NNN/` for the dashboard. You:
266	
267	1. **Read** the full report (the wrapper's stdout summary is compressed).
268	2. **Spot-check** load-bearing claims (the routine sorry-count / compile checks are already done by the loop).
269	3. **Update STRATEGY.md** if findings change the long-arc plan.
270	4. **Update PROGRESS.md** with whatever new objectives the report enables.
271	
272	### Canonical ordering
273	
274	Within a plan phase: read-only critics / precedent consults first, write-capable subagents next, verification / envelope subagents last. **Write prover objectives only after** the subagents have stabilized the definitional landscape.
275	
276	You may invoke a subagent multiple times per iter (distinct slugs each call) when justified.
277	
278	## Informal content for the prover
279	
280	The prover does much better with rich informal guidance. Before assigning a task, ensure the prover has access to the relevant informal proof.
281	
282	- **Short hints** (a few sentences): in `PROGRESS.md` under the objective.
283	- **Medium content** (a paragraph or two): in the corresponding `.lean` file as a `/- ... -/` block above the declaration.
284	- **Long content** (full sketch, paper summary, multi-step construction): in the blueprint chapter `.tex`.
285	- **When a reference is vague**: actually consult the source before assigning the task. Options (pick whichever your environment provides):
286	  - The auto-injected subagent catalog — a literature/reference-fetching subagent surfaces there when enabled and downloads the original source files (PDF + TeX when available) into `references/`, each with a pointer `.md` index card.
287	  - `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` directly when you only need to confirm a paper exists or read a short passage.
288	  - `archon-informal-agent.py` to ask an external LLM for a proof-style sketch — **only when API credentials are configured** (`OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`); the output is LLM-generated, NOT source-derived, so don't treat it as a literature cross-check.
289	  - Never send the prover in blind, and never synthesize a "literature cross-check" from your own context — see the anti-fabrication rule below.
290	
291	Always record in `PROGRESS.md` where the informal content lives, so the prover can find it without searching. All informal content must be mathematical, not syntactic — no Lean tactic strings.
292	
293	## Anti-fabrication rule (applies to all verification work)
294	
295	When a hint or strategy step asks for verification against an external source — literature cross-check, citation lookup, "consult the paper", "verify the construction matches Hartshorne III.6", a request to invoke a specific tool, etc. — and the named tool or path can't actually execute (missing API credentials, paywall, broken environment, source not found, tool reports `NOT_FOUND`), **you MUST NOT synthesize the verification output from your own context**. The planner's context is the same context that produced the claims being verified; a planner-written cross-check is circular by construction and worse than skipping the verification, because it disguises absence of verification as presence of it.
296	
297	The acceptable responses, in order of preference:
298	
299	1. **Substitute with an equivalent.** If the user named a specific tool (e.g. `archon-informal-agent.py`) but that tool can't run, look at the auto-injected subagent catalog above for a subagent that performs equivalent work (e.g. a literature/reference-fetching subagent for a literature request), or use `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` directly. Record the substitution in `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` under a `## Tool substitutions` section so the user sees what you did and can correct the hint if the substitution is wrong.
300	
301	2. **Partial verification + honest scope.** If you can verify some claims but not others (some seeds resolve, some don't), surface that explicitly: which claims are verified, against which sources, and which remain unverified. The downstream blueprint-writer should cite only the verified ones; the unverified ones stay flagged.
302	
303	3. **Escalate to the user.** When neither substitution nor partial verification is possible, append a one-line bullet to `USER_HINTS.md` naming the specific failure (e.g. *"archon-informal-agent.py has no API credentials in env — please set `OPENAI_API_KEY`, or rephrase the hint to allow a different tool"*), and proceed with the iter WITHOUT the verification, flagging in `PROGRESS.md` that this iter's strategic decisions affected by the missing verification are unverified.
304	
305	What you may NEVER do: write a file named `references/<topic>-crosscheck.md` (or similar) whose content is your own synthesis, dressed up to look like a verification report. If a future planner or prover treats that file as ground truth, they're acting on circular evidence the project has no way to detect or correct. This is the failure mode this rule exists to prevent — assume any "I'll just write it myself from what I remember" impulse is wrong, and use one of the three acceptable responses above instead.
306	
307	## Prover failure modes
308	
309	- **"Mathlib doesn't have it"** — the #1 failure. Do not pass it back with "try harder". Find an alternative route via the catalog (a literature/reference-fetching subagent when enabled), `WebSearch`/`WebFetch`, or `archon-informal-agent.py` for a proof-style sketch when its API credentials are configured. If the gap is in a definition, dispatch a write-capable structural subagent from your catalog. Update the chapter `.tex` with the re-routed proof before reassigning.
310	- **Wrong construction** — instruct revert (single file) or dispatch a structural subagent (cross-file). Update the chapter first.
311	- **Not using Web Search** — explicitly instruct: "use Web Search to find [arXiv ID], decompose into sub-lemmas, formalize step by step". Update the chapter with the retrieved sketch.
312	- **Early stop on a hard problem** — reject the report. Break into sub-goals in the chapter, assign L1, then L2 after L1 lands.
313	- **Tricks to bypass** (new axioms, ad-hoc weakenings) — reject. Document why this route was chosen and ensure it won't reproduce.
314	- **Repeated blockers** — same blocker over consecutive iters means rewrite the chapter or dispatch a structural subagent. Do NOT re-dispatch the same lane with cosmetic recipe variation.
315	
316	## Verification
317	
318	The loop already runs deterministic checks each iter:
319	
320	- **Sorry count** — stamped into `meta.json` (before/mid/post prover). Do not re-count by hand.
321	- **Axiom check** — runs as part of the blueprint-doctor; new axioms surface in your injected findings block.
322	- **Blueprint consistency** — `sync_leanok` resolves `\lean{...}` against the project decls; the doctor catches broken `\ref` / `\uses`.
323	
324	What's left for you: spot-check inconsistent prover self-reports; act on every entry in the injected doctor findings (or document the deferral); reject any reported completion that left a real `sorry` or introduced a new axiom.
325	
326	## Decomposition strategy
327	
328	When a prover is stuck on a large theorem: read the chapter for sub-lemma structure (L1, L2, …); read related `references/` to align with the original proof; expand the chapter if too thin (dispatch a blueprint-writing or literature-fetching subagent from the catalog, or use `WebSearch`/`WebFetch` directly); assign one sub-lemma at a time; verify, then assign the next; record each sub-lemma's status in `PROGRESS.md`.
329	
330	## Soundness check before spending budget
331	
332	**Churning and unsoundness are different signals — the critic catches the first, only you catch the second.** A recurring blocker is not always a hard-but-true gap; sometimes the target `sorry` is a statement that is simply **false as written** (a missing hypothesis, a wrong quantifier, an unstated connectedness/finiteness assumption). Pouring prover budget into a false statement burns iterations forever — the prover correctly cannot close it, the progress-critic reports CHURNING, and everyone treats it as a Mathlib gap when the real fix is one word in the statement.
333	
334	So **before committing more than one iter of prover budget to a hard or recurring `sorry`** (a target the progress-critic flags as a repeated blocker, or any target you estimate at multiple iters / >~100 LOC), first spend a cheap pass trying to **DISPROVE** the statement:
335	
336	- Instantiate it on the smallest non-trivial models — finite, degenerate, or boundary cases (e.g. for an algebra claim: `B = k × k`, `B = ℚ(√2)`, the zero ring, a one-point space). Does any satisfy the hypotheses but violate the conclusion?
337	- If you can't see it yourself, dispatch the informal / mathlib-analogist subagent from your catalog with a directive that asks specifically for a **counterexample or a satisfiability sketch**, not a proof.
338	- Check whether the source the statement claims to follow actually states it with the same hypotheses (cite-and-read discipline applies — read the local source file).
339	
340	If a counterexample turns up, the statement (or a missing hypothesis) is the bug: fix the blueprint statement, mark the Lean declaration with a `% NOTE:` for review, and do NOT assign a prover to formalize the false version. If the disproof attempt fails, you've cheaply raised your confidence that the target is true — now spend the budget. Record the disproof attempt and its outcome in the iter sidecar so the next planner doesn't repeat it.
341	
342	## Multi-agent coordination
343	
344	Provers run in parallel — one per file. Number objectives clearly; each maps to exactly one `.lean` file. Reference the blueprint chapter alongside, and **list every ready sorry in that file that the prover should fill in this iter** — not just one:
345	
346	```markdown
347	## Current Objectives
348	
349	1. **`Core.lean`** — Fill sorries in `filter_convergence` (line 156), `filter_inv` (line 188), `filter_assoc` (line 211). Blueprint: `chapters/Core.tex` (`thm:filter_convergence`, `thm:filter_inv`, `thm:filter_assoc`).
350	2. **`Measure.lean`** — Fill sorry in `sigma_finite_restrict` (line 45). Blueprint: `chapters/Measure.tex`.
351	3. **`ChartAlgebra.lean`** — Scaffold the file with declarations for `thm:chart_id`, `thm:chart_comp`, `thm:chart_inv` from the chapter; leave bodies as `sorry`. Blueprint: `chapters/ChartAlgebra.tex`. (File-skeleton dispatch — see below.)
352	```
353	
354	**Agent count = file count.** The dispatcher fans out one prover per file. When a file has multiple ready sorries, list ALL of them under that file's objective — the prover handles them sequentially within one lane. Splitting a multi-sorry file across iters is artificial throttling; one lane working sequentially on three sorries finishes faster than waiting two iters for three single-sorry lanes (the prover keeps its file context warm across sorries).
355	
356	**File-skeleton dispatches.** When a blueprint chapter is complete but the corresponding `.lean` file does not yet exist (or exists but is missing declarations the chapter introduced), it is a legitimate iter objective to dispatch a prover with directive *"scaffold `Foo.lean` with declarations for `thm:a`, `thm:b`, `thm:c` from the chapter; leave bodies as `sorry`; add the import + namespace boilerplate; do not attempt to prove anything yet"*. The next iter then fills the sorries. This is materially faster than one-iter-per-declaration scaffolding.
357	
358	**Mechanical-vs-deep partition.** Sorries split into two regimes:
359	- *Mechanical* — typeclass wiring, instance synthesis, ring-level algebra, simp/ring-tactic territory, definitional unfolding glue. A prover lane can comfortably close 3–6 of these in one iter (the attempt-cap permits each one a fresh budget). When the upcoming work is mechanical, load lanes aggressively.
360	- *Deep* — the load-bearing categorical / geometric / analytic argument. One per lane, often less. The prover may also legitimately spend an iter exploring without closing anything.
361	
362	Use this partition to decide how thickly to load each lane. Don't load a deep lane with three deep sorries; that just thrashes the prover's attempt budget across all three. Don't restrict a mechanical lane to one sorry "to keep it simple"; the prover wants the batched objectives.
363	
364	Balance difficulty so all provers finish around the same time. Avoid shallow / trivial objectives unless they unblock something downstream this iter. Don't artificially throttle — the prover prompt says "push as far as possible"; your objective list must give it room to.
365	
366	If a previous experiment is being restarted, check compilation status of every target `.lean` first. Prioritize files with sorries or compile errors; don't redo completed work.
367	
368	**Dispatch cap.** The runner refuses to fan out more than ~10 provers in a single iter (configurable via `--max-objectives`, default 10). Writing 15+ files into `## Current Objectives` is a planning failure, not a tooling limitation — even when the project has many open files, the right iter-level move is to pick the most urgent ≤10 (mechanical lanes counted) and defer the rest to the next iter. If the deterministic plan-validate guard truncates your list, the surplus is added to `USER_HINTS.md` so the next planner sees what got deferred. Don't rely on the safety net — pick the right ~10 the first time.
369	
370	**Blocked-deps filter.** Before dispatch, plan-validate also drops any objective whose transitive local imports failed the *previous* `lake build`. Reason: a prover assigned to `Downstream.lean` that imports `Upstream.lean`-which-doesn't-compile would fail to even load the file, burning API time for nothing. The blocked set is parsed from `.archon/last_lake_build.log`. There is one important exception: a blocked file that's *itself* an objective this iter is presumed-being-fixed — the planner is allowed to assign `Upstream.lean` and `Downstream.lean` together (the prover phase handles them in import order). When the filter drops files, they're listed in `USER_HINTS.md` with their specific blocking deps, so you can prioritize fixing the upstream files next iter. Best practice: when you see `## Build state` flagging compile errors, put those files at the top of `## Current Objectives` so the filter exempts the downstream lanes that depend on them.
371	
372	## Dependency graph
373	
374	Optional but cheap. Before scoping objectives, you may run:
375	
376	```
377	${LEAN4_PYTHON_BIN:-python3} "$LEAN4_SCRIPTS/dependency_graph.py" . --format=summary
378	```
379	
380	It parses imports + chapter `\lean{...}` / `\uses{...}` / `\proves{...}` / markers and emits a project-wide view in under a second. Use it to order objectives — upstream files first, downstream files later.
381

386	- `autoformalize` → `prover` (all statements formalized)
387	- `prover` → `polish` (all sorries filled and verified)
388	- `polish` → `COMPLETE` (proofs clean, compile)

209	2. (⇐) Step 1: `range i.base = i.ker.support` (via `range_subschemeι`

217	     ≤ vanishingIdeal g.ker.support = g.ker.radical = g.ker`.
218	6. Step 6: apply `IsClosedImmersion.lift`.

# Strategy

## Goal

Formalize Christian Merten's Jacobian challenge (`references/challenge.lean`):
nine protected declarations headlined by `AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian` and
`Jacobian.nonempty_jacobianWitness` — existence of an Albanese / Jacobian
object uniform over the `k`-rational pointing of a smooth proper
geometrically irreducible curve `C / k`, with **no** `C(k) ≠ ∅` hypothesis.
End-state: zero inline `sorry`, kernel-only axioms (`propext`,
`Classical.choice`, `Quot.sound`). **Char-general:** the protected
signatures take `[Field k]` only — NO `CharZero`.

The witness OBJECT `J` is always real (constructed unconditionally); only
`isAlbaneseFor` is universally quantified over `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`. Spine:
**pointed vs. unpointed**. `genus C := \dim_k H^1(C, O_C)` (arithmetic
genus; protected — cannot be re-typed).

## Phases & estimations

| Phase | Status | Iters left | LOC (remaining · realized/it) | Key Mathlib needs | Risks |

| **A.1.a — `RelativeSpec`** | functionally complete; signature refinement separately tracked | ~5–10 | ~80–150 · ~0/it | qcoh-algebras → schemes | signature drift |

1	# Analogy: Lane B substrate — `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` and tensor reducedness

12	## Question
13	
14	For the Lane B (`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01`) iter-188 (III.c)
15	separated-locus structural setup, the substantive residual is a pair-
16	morphism factorization through the diagonal closed immersion. The
17	blueprint cites `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` as the
18	substrate; iter-188 verified this is NOT in Mathlib at b80f227. Two
19	questions:
20	
21	1. Is there a Mathlib variant of "morphism factors through closed
22	   immersion iff topological range subset" under any reasonable
23	   reduced-source hypothesis?
24	2. What is the cleanest project-side construction (with LOC), and what
25	   blocks the tensor-reducedness gap that also obstructs the other two
26	   off-target sorries (`gm_geomIrred`, `projGm_isReduced`)?
27	
28	## Project artifact(s)
29	
30	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean:417-553` —
31	  `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01` (the cocycle proof with the
32	  separated-locus structural setup landed iter-188; substantive sorry
33	  at line 553).
34	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean:755-763` —
35	  `gm_geomIrred` (off-target sorry, same tensor gap).
36	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean:779-795` —
37	  `projGm_isReduced` (off-target sorry, same tensor gap).
38	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/Points.lean:178-229` —
39	  `GmRing = Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)` and
40	  `gmRing_isDomain`.
41	- `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex:1505-1611` —
42	  (III.c) Separated-locus alternative recipe, including the iter-188

45	## Decisions identified
46	
47	### Decision 1: `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` (the substantive substrate)
48	
49	- **Mathlib idiom**: NONE at b80f227. The only `lift` variant shipped
50	  is `IsClosedImmersion.lift` with the kernel-inequality form
51	  (`f.ker ≤ g.ker`), at
52	  `.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/ClosedImmersion.lean:200-214`.
53	  The `lift_iff_range_subset` variant — equating factor-through with
54	  set-theoretic range containment under a reduced-source hypothesis —
55	  is not in Mathlib (verified iter-188 via `lean_leansearch` and
56	  re-verified iter-189 via `lean_leanfinder`).
57	
58	- **Mathlib substrate that DOES exist** (and which a project-side build
59	  would chain): the iter-189 search recovered the complete chain of
60	  primitives needed:
61	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.ker`
62	    (`.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/IdealSheaf/Basic.lean:689`):
63	    ideal-sheaf kernel of a scheme morphism.
64	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.ker_apply`
65	    (same file, `:698-734`): for `[QuasiCompact f]`,
66	    `f.ker.ideal U = RingHom.ker (f.app U).hom` on each affine open.
67	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.support_ker`
68	    (same file, `:839-878`): for `[QuasiCompact f]`,
69	    `f.ker.support = closure (Set.range f)`.
70	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.range_subset_ker_support`
71	    (same file, `:754-764`): the unconditional inclusion
72	    `Set.range f ⊆ f.ker.support`.
73	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support`
74	    (same file, `:631-639`):
75	    `vanishingIdeal I.support = I.radical` — the Galois-connection
76	    pinning of "support determines radical".
77	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.gc`
78	    (same file, `:622-625`):
79	    `GaloisConnection (support ·) (vanishingIdeal ·)` (with the
80	    `Closedsᵒᵈ` ordering).
81	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.range_subschemeι`
82	    (`IdealSheaf/Subscheme.lean`):
83	    `Set.range I.subschemeι.base = I.support` — for a closed immersion
84	    `i`, `Set.range i.base = i.ker.support`.
85	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.IsClosedImmersion.lift`,
86	    `lift_fac`, `lift_fac_assoc`
87	    (`ClosedImmersion.lean:206-214`): the actual lift,
88	    keyed on `f.ker ≤ g.ker`.
89	
90	- **Project's current path**: iter-188 lands a structural setup that
91	  introduces `s_pair := pullback.lift (chart0_via_fst) (chart1_via_snd) hPLB_agree`
92	  and the closed-immersion diagonal `hΔ`, leaving a single direct
93	  `sorry` at line 553 where the (III.c) recipe step 3 needs the
94	  factorization `∃ s, s ≫ Δ = s_pair`. The blueprint cites
95	  `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` to reduce this to the
96	  set-level containment of the cocycle's image in `Set.range Δ.base`.
97	
98	- **Gap**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (the Mathlib idiom doesn't exist),
99	  but the gap is BUILDABLE from existing Mathlib primitives.
100	
101	- **Cost of divergence**: Building `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset`
102	  project-side costs ~40-60 LOC; the alternative (waiting on Mathlib
103	  upstream PR) is unbounded in time.
104	
105	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL** — and the gap-fill is
106	  feasible at ~40-60 LOC using the existing Mathlib primitives above.
107	
108	### Decision 2: Tensor reducedness of `(Away (X_0·X_1)) ⊗_kbar (GmRing kbar)`
109	
110	This is the SECOND substrate needed, blocking three sorries
111	simultaneously (the cocycle factorization plus the two off-target
112	`gm_geomIrred` / `projGm_isReduced` instances at L755, L779).
113	
114	- **Mathlib idiom check**: NONE direct. The closest shipped:
115	  - `Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced` class
116	    (`Mathlib/RingTheory/Nilpotent/GeometricallyReduced.lean:47`):
117	    requires `IsReduced (AlgebraicClosure k ⊗_k A)`. The bridge
118	    "k alg-closed ⟹ IsReduced ⟹ IsGeometricallyReduced k" is NOT
119	    shipped (the file's own TODO confirms: "for every field extension
120	    K of k the K-algebra K ⊗[k] A is reduced" — not landed for the
121	    general field-extension case).
122	  - `Algebra.instIsReducedTensorProductOfIsAlgebraicOfIsGeometricallyReduced`
123	    (same file, `:52`): an instance for `IsReduced (K ⊗_k A)` when
124	    `K/k` algebraic AND `A` geometrically reduced over `k`. Doesn't
125	    apply directly because neither factor here is algebraic over kbar.
126	  - `Subalgebra.LinearDisjoint.isDomain_of_injective`
127	    (`Mathlib/RingTheory/LinearDisjoint.lean`): the abstract route
128	    "tensor of two domains embedding into a common domain with
129	    linearly-disjoint ranges is a domain". Building the LinearDisjoint
130	    witness for our concrete factors would be its own ~30-50 LOC
131	    sub-task.
132	
133	- **The clean project path** (the analogist's recommended route):
134	  exploit Mathlib's **localization–polynomial tensor product
135	  iso machinery**. The relevant Mathlib lemmas, both shipped:
136	  - `IsLocalization.Away.tensorRightEquiv`
137	    (`Mathlib/RingTheory/Localization/BaseChange.lean`):
138	    `TensorProduct R A S ≃ₐ[S] Localization.Away ((algebraMap R S) r)`
139	    when `A = Localization.Away r` over `R`. Tensor of a localization
140	    with anything is a localization (in the tensored ring).
141	  - `MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv`
142	    (`Mathlib/RingTheory/TensorProduct/MvPolynomial.lean`):
143	    `TensorProduct R A (MvPolynomial σ R) ≃ₐ[A] MvPolynomial σ A`.
144	    Tensor with a polynomial ring over the base is just polynomials
145	    over the algebra.
146	
147	  Both factors of our tensor are localizations of polynomial rings
148	  over kbar:
149	  - `GmRing kbar = Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`
150	    — i.e., `kbar[t][1/t]`.
151	  - `Away (X_0·X_1) = HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projLineBarGrading) (X_0·X_1)`
152	    — a kbar-algebra that is a domain (already proven in
153	    `projectiveLineBar_isReduced` chain via the `val_injective`
154	    embedding into `Localization.Away (X_0·X_1)`).
155	
156	  Composition:
157	  1. `Away (X_0·X_1) ⊗_kbar kbar[t] ≃ MvPolynomial Unit (Away (X_0·X_1))`
158	     via `MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv`.
159	  2. The localization `kbar[t] → kbar[t][1/t] = GmRing` corresponds via
160	     `IsScalarTower` + `IsLocalization.Away.tensorRightEquiv` to
161	     the localization at the same generator's image in the tensored
162	     polynomial ring.
163	  3. Result: `Away (X_0·X_1) ⊗_kbar GmRing ≃ Localization.Away (X () in MvPolynomial Unit (Away (X_0·X_1)))`.
164	  4. `MvPolynomial Unit (Away (X_0·X_1))` is a polynomial ring over a
165	     domain, hence a domain.
166	  5. Localization at powers of `X ()` (a non-zero divisor in the
167	     polynomial ring over a domain) preserves the domain property
168	     via `IsLocalization.isDomain_localization` +
169	     `powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors`
170	     + `MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero` (already in the project's playbook,
171	     used in `projectiveLineBar_isReduced` L734-738).
172	
173	  Therefore `IsDomain (Away (X_0·X_1) ⊗_kbar GmRing)` follows, and
174	  `IsReduced` follows from `IsDomain` directly.
175	
176	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL** — buildable at ~50-80 LOC.
177	  No new Mathlib infrastructure needed; everything is a chain of
178	  existing iso lemmas. The same pattern unifies the `gm_geomIrred` /
179	  `projGm_isReduced` closures (same generators, different chart degrees).
180	
181	### Decision 3: Where to land the substrate
182	
183	- **Recommended file layout**:
184	  - New helper file: `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/Cross01Substrate.lean`
185	    importing `BareScheme`, `ChartIso`, `Points`. Contains:
186	    - `tensor_AwayGm_isDomain` (the tensor reducedness lemma, generic
187	      in the homogeneous generator's degree).
188	    - `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` (the substrate lemma,
189	      generic in the schemes).
190	  - Cross01 cocycle body lifts into `GmScaling.lean` (no file move
191	    needed — the substrate is reused from `Cross01Substrate.lean`).
192	
193	- **Why a new file vs inline in `GmScaling.lean`**:
194	  - The substrate is generic (no `gmScalingP1`-specific dependencies).
195	  - The substrate is reused by `gm_geomIrred` and `projGm_isReduced`
196	    (current `sorry`s in the SAME file), so a shared private namespace
197	    in `Cross01Substrate.lean` avoids duplication.
198	  - The substrate may be promotable to Mathlib later (the
199	    `lift_iff_range_subset` variant is a natural addition to
200	    `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.ClosedImmersion`).
201	
202	- **Verdict**: **BUILD_PROJECT_HELPER** in a new file `Cross01Substrate.lean`.

255	## Concrete project-side recipe
256	
257	### Substrate 1: `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset`
258	
259	Target signature:
260	
261	```lean
262	theorem AlgebraicGeometry.IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset
263	    {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (i : Z ⟶ X) [IsClosedImmersion i] [IsReduced Z]
264	    (g : Y ⟶ X) [QuasiCompact g] [IsReduced Y] :
265	    (∃ h : Y ⟶ Z, h ≫ i = g) ↔
266	      Set.range g.base ⊆ Set.range i.base
267	```
268	
269	Body skeleton (with `≈ 40-60` LOC):
270	
271	```lean
272	  constructor
273	  · -- (⇒) Forward direction: trivial range pushdown.
274	    rintro ⟨h, rfl⟩
275	    intro x ⟨y, rfl⟩
276	    exact ⟨h.base y, rfl⟩
277	  · -- (⇐) Backward direction: the substance.
278	    intro hrange
279	    -- Step 1: closed immersion ⟹ range is closed, equal to ker.support.
280	    have hi_range : Set.range i.base = (i.ker.support : Set X) := by
281	      rw [← IdealSheafData.range_subschemeι]
282	      -- Use that i = subschemeι i.ker (up to iso) via toImage_imageι
283	      sorry  -- ~5 LOC bridge using Scheme.Hom.toImage / imageι
284	    -- Step 2: g.ker.support = closure (range g) ⊆ i.ker.support
285	    --         (latter is closed).
286	    have hsup : g.ker.support ≤ i.ker.support := by
287	      rw [Hom.support_ker]
288	      refine closure_minimal ?_ i.ker.isClosed_supportSet
289	      exact hrange.trans hi_range.le
290	    -- Step 3: Y reduced ⟹ g.ker is radical
291	    --         (since g.ker.ideal U = RingHom.ker (g.app U)
292	    --          and Y reduced ⟹ that kernel is radical).
293	    have hg_rad : g.ker.radical = g.ker := by
294	      ext U : 2
295	      simp only [IdealSheafData.radical_ideal, g.ker_apply]
296	      exact (RingHom.ker_isRadical_iff_reduced_of_surjective
297	        Function.Surjective.rfl).mpr inferInstance  -- needs adjustment
298	      -- Concretely: use `f.app U`'s kernel-is-radical because Y is reduced;
299	      -- the appropriate lemma is `RingHom.ker_isRadical_of_reduced_codomain`.
300	      sorry  -- ~10 LOC; standard "reduced source ⟹ kernel of ring map radical"
301	    -- Step 4: Z reduced ⟹ i.ker is radical (same argument).
302	    have hi_rad : i.ker.radical = i.ker := by
303	      ext U : 2
304	      sorry  -- ~5 LOC, symmetric to Step 3
305	    -- Step 5: combine via Galois connection.
306	    --         g.ker.support ⊆ i.ker.support
307	    --         ⟹ vanishingIdeal(i.ker.support) ≤ vanishingIdeal(g.ker.support)
308	    --         ⟹ i.ker.radical ≤ g.ker.radical (by `vanishingIdeal_support`)
309	    --         ⟹ i.ker ≤ g.ker (by Steps 3, 4).
310	    have hker : i.ker ≤ g.ker := by
311	      calc i.ker
312	          = i.ker.radical := hi_rad.symm
313	        _ = vanishingIdeal i.ker.support := vanishingIdeal_support.symm
314	        _ ≤ vanishingIdeal g.ker.support := vanishingIdeal_antimono hsup
315	        _ = g.ker.radical := vanishingIdeal_support
316	        _ = g.ker := hg_rad
317	    -- Step 6: apply Mathlib's IsClosedImmersion.lift.
318	    exact ⟨IsClosedImmersion.lift i g hker,
319	           IsClosedImmersion.lift_fac i g hker⟩
320	```
321	
322	**Notes on the LOC**:
323	- The two "kernel-is-radical" steps (3, 4) might need a Mathlib bridge
324	  lemma not directly searched here; if so, that adds ~10-15 LOC for an
325	  inline derivation (use `Hom.ker_apply` to drop to ring kernels, then
326	  reducedness of the source gives radicality of the ring kernel — a
327	  standard `RingHom.ker_isRadical` argument).
328	- The Step 1 bridge (closed immersion `i` is the `subschemeι` of its
329	  kernel, up to iso) uses `Scheme.Hom.toImage_imageι` from Mathlib.
330	
331	**Total**: ~40-60 LOC.

333	### Substrate 2: tensor reducedness `(Away (X_0·X_1)) ⊗_kbar GmRing`
334	
335	Target signature (generic in the homogeneous generator `f` of degree `d`):
336	
337	```lean
338	theorem AlgebraicGeometry.gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain
339	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] {d : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d)
340	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
341	    (hf : f ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar d)
342	    (hf_ne : f ≠ 0) :
343	    IsDomain (TensorProduct kbar
344	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f)
345	      (GmRing kbar))
346	```
347	
348	Body skeleton (with `≈ 50-80` LOC):
349	
350	```lean
351	  -- Step 1: Away 𝒜 f is a domain (project-side, used in
352	  -- projectiveLineBar_isReduced L734-738).
353	  haveI hf_dom : IsDomain (HomogeneousLocalization.Away
354	      (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f) := by
355	    refine Function.Injective.isDomain
356	      (algebraMap (HomogeneousLocalization.Away _ f) (Localization.Away f)) ?_
357	    · intro x y h
358	      exact HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective _ h
359	  -- Step 2: Build the iso chain.
360	  -- (a) MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv :
361	  --     TensorProduct kbar (Away 𝒜 f) (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ≃ₐ[Away 𝒜 f]
362	  --       MvPolynomial Unit (Away 𝒜 f)
363	  -- (b) IsScalarTower over kbar lifts the localization at X () in MvPoly Unit kbar
364	  --     to the corresponding localization at X () in MvPoly Unit (Away 𝒜 f).
365	  -- (c) Resulting iso:
366	  --     TensorProduct kbar (Away 𝒜 f) (GmRing kbar) ≃
367	  --       Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit (Away 𝒜 f))
368	  -- Step 3: RHS is a domain.
369	  -- MvPolynomial Unit (Away 𝒜 f) is a polynomial ring over a domain,
370	  -- hence a domain (instance auto-resolves).
371	  -- Localization at powers of X () (a non-zero divisor, by
372	  -- MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero) preserves the domain property via
373	  -- IsLocalization.isDomain_localization + powers_le_nonZeroDivisors.
374	  sorry  -- ~50-80 LOC threading the iso chain and applying domain instances
375	```
376	
377	The body's main work is threading `IsLocalization.Away.tensorRightEquiv`
378	(or its symmetric `tensorEquiv` variant) at the `kbar[t] → GmRing`
379	localization step, after collapsing the `Away 𝒜 f ⊗_kbar kbar[t]` tensor
380	via `algebraTensorAlgEquiv`. The threading requires careful instance
381	management (`IsScalarTower kbar (Away 𝒜 f) (MvPolynomial Unit (Away 𝒜 f))`,
382	the `Algebra` from polynomial ring over an algebra, the `IsLocalization.Away`
383	instance on the tensored side) — accounting for the upper end of the
384	50-80 LOC range.
385	
386	**Corollary**:
387	```lean
388	instance : IsReduced (Spec (.of (TensorProduct kbar (Away _ f) (GmRing kbar))))
389	```
390	follows in ~3 LOC via `inferInstanceAs (IsReduced (Spec ...))` after the
391	domain instance lands.
392	
393	### Application 1: cocycle body completion
394	
395	The iter-188 structural setup already lands the harness in
396	`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01`:
397	- `s_pair`, `hs_fst`, `hs_snd`, `hΔ`, `hsep`, `hPLB_agree` are all built.
398	- Goal at line 553 is reducible to `∃ s, s ≫ Δ = s_pair`.
399	
400	With Substrates 1+2 in hand:
401	1. Establish `IsReduced (intersection)` by transporting the `Spec` domain
402	   instance across `gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso` (~10 LOC).
403	2. Establish the range containment `range s_pair.base ⊆ range Δ.base`
404	   by closed-points check (each closed point of intersection lies over
405	   a single kbar-point of `D_+(X_0·X_1) × Gm`, both chart-maps evaluate
406	   to the same PLB-point) — ~15-25 LOC via point-checking on closed points
407	   (kbar-rational since kbar is alg-closed and intersection is locally
408	   of finite type).
409	3. Apply `lift_iff_range_subset` to extract `s`, then close via
410	   `pullback.diagonal_fst` / `_snd` (= 𝟙) — ~10 LOC.
411	
412	**Total application**: ~35-50 LOC.
413	
414	### Application 2: bonus sorries
415	
416	The same Substrate 2 closes:
417	- `projGm_isReduced` (~15-25 LOC): cover `(PLB ⊗ Gm).left` by the
418	  per-chart `Spec (Away X_i ⊗ GmRing)` (using the existing
419	  `gmScalingP1_cover`), each reduced by Substrate 2 at the degree-1
420	  generator; conclude via `IsReduced.of_openCover`.
421	- `gm_geomIrred` (~15-30 LOC): for `[IsAlgClosed kbar]`, every algebraic
422	  extension K/kbar is isomorphic to kbar (up to alg-closure equivalence),
423	  so the base-change `GmRing ⊗_kbar K = Localization.Away t in K[t]`
424	  is a domain → `Spec` is irreducible. The Mathlib bridge
425	  `Algebra.instIsReducedTensorProductOfIsAlgebraicOfIsGeometricallyReduced`
426	  (line 52 of `GeometricallyReduced.lean`) MIGHT apply once
427	  `GmRing kbar` is shown geometrically reduced — that's a separate
428	  ~10-15 LOC argument.
429	
430	**Total bonus**: ~30-55 LOC for both.
431	
432	## Total LOC estimate
433	
434	| Sub-task | LOC | Substrate or application? |
435	|---|---|---|
436	| Substrate 1: `lift_iff_range_subset` | 40–60 | substrate (Cross01Substrate.lean) |
437	| Substrate 2: tensor reducedness | 50–80 | substrate (Cross01Substrate.lean) |
438	| Application: cocycle body | 35–50 | inline in GmScaling.lean |
439	| Bonus: `projGm_isReduced` + `gm_geomIrred` | 30–55 | inline in GmScaling.lean |
440	
441	**Total**: ~155–245 LOC over 3–5 iters.
442	
443	This matches the iter-188 escalation's ~150-200 LOC estimate at the
444	lower end and slightly exceeds at the upper end. The substrate work is
445	front-loaded (Substrates 1+2 = ~90-140 LOC in iters 189-190); the
446	application work is rapid follow-on (iters 191-193).
447	
448	## Hard binary verdict
449	
450	**(B) FEASIBLE at 80-200 LOC.**
451	
452	The substrate (Substrate 1 + Substrate 2 combined) lands at
453	~90-140 LOC; the cocycle application closes within 35-50 LOC; the
454	bonus sorries close within 30-55 LOC. Total ranges 155-245 LOC, with
455	the central estimate at ~180-200 LOC.
456	
457	**Verdict A (FEASIBLE at <80 LOC) is ruled out**: Mathlib does not
458	ship `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` and there is no
459	shorter Mathlib-direct route. The substrate is genuine work, not a
460	naming-only gap.
461	
462	**Verdict C (NOT FEASIBLE at <200 LOC, USER ESCALATION) is NOT triggered**:
463	the work is bounded and uses only existing Mathlib primitives. No
464	upstream Mathlib PR is required. The Galois-connection chain
465	(`vanishingIdeal ↔ support` with `Hom.support_ker` and
466	`range_subschemeι`) is the canonical Stacks-style path and is fully
467	formalized in Mathlib.
468	
469	## Recommendation
470	
471	Proceed with **Option B (project-side substrate)** as planned. Land
472	the work in a new file `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/Cross01Substrate.lean`
473	containing the two substrate lemmas, imported by `GmScaling.lean`.
474	
475	**Phased delivery (3-5 iters)**:
476	- **Iter 189**: dispatch a prover on `Cross01Substrate.lean` to land
477	  Substrate 1 (`lift_iff_range_subset`); this is the smaller chunk
478	  (~40-60 LOC) and unblocks the application path independently.
479	- **Iter 190**: dispatch a prover on `Cross01Substrate.lean` to land
480	  Substrate 2 (tensor reducedness); this is the larger chunk
481	  (~50-80 LOC) and uses Mathlib's iso-chain machinery
482	  (`MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv` + `IsLocalization.Away.tensorRightEquiv`).
483	- **Iter 191-193**: dispatch a prover on `GmScaling.lean` to close
484	  the three open sorries (`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01`,
485	  `gm_geomIrred`, `projGm_isReduced`) using both substrates.
486	
487	**Reversal trigger**: if iter-190's tensor reducedness substrate
488	fails to type-check at the `tensorRightEquiv` step due to a
489	`Algebra kbar ...` / `IsScalarTower` instance mismatch, fall back to
490	the `Subalgebra.LinearDisjoint.isDomain_of_injective` route (build
491	the linear-disjoint witness via the polynomial-ring-disjoint-variables
492	classical fact). Same LOC budget; different elaboration shape.

1	\chapter{Relative Spec}
2	\label{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelativeSpec.lean
4	
5	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-172). This chapter is the foundational A.1.a sub-row of
6	% Route A (positive-genus arm of nonempty_jacobianWitness). It packages the
7	% relative-spectrum functor \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) : \mathrm{QcohAlg}(X)^{op}
8	% \to \mathrm{Sch}/X used by the relative Picard functor on a product C \times_k T.
9	% Mathlib has the absolute Spec functor and affine gluing (Scheme.GlueData,
10	% AffineScheme.glueOpens) but no general RelativeSpec construction; this chapter is
11	% the prover-ready specification for the new file AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelativeSpec.lean
12	% which iter-173 (file-skeleton lane) will scaffold.
13	
14	\section{Setup and motivation}
15	\label{sec:relspec_setup}
16	
17	The Route~A construction of \(J = \Pic^0_{C/k}\) as a \(g\)-dimensional abelian variety is
18	organised around the relative Picard functor
19	\(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T) = \Pic(C \times_k T) / \pi_T^* \Pic(T)\)
20	on the category of \(k\)-schemes. Representing \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\) requires the line-bundle
21	pullback construction on a relative curve \(C \times_k T \to T\), which in turn rests on a
22	\emph{relative scheme} construction on a quasi-coherent sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebras
23	\(\mathcal{A}\), the \emph{relative spectrum}
24	\(\pi : \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \to X\). This chapter packages the construction
25	of \(\underline{\Spec}_X(-)\) and its three core properties --- universal property, affine
26	base, base change --- as the foundational A.1.a building block. The relative Picard
27	functor itself (A.1.c), the line-bundle pullback functor on a product (A.1.b), and the
28	identity-component / dimension theory of \(J\) (A.3) live in separate sibling chapters
29	(\S~``Out of scope'' below).
30	
31	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Section ``Relative spectrum via glueing'' and ``Relative
32	% spectrum as a functor'' (tags 01LL, 01LO, 01LQ, 01LR, 01LS, available locally at
33	% references/stacks-constructions.tex L309-L720). The Hartshorne reference is
34	% [Hartshorne], II Exercise 5.17 (read from references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf).
35	
36	\section{Quasi-coherent sheaves of algebras}
37	\label{sec:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
38	
39	\begin{definition}\leanok
40	[Quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebra]
41	  \label{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
42	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QcohAlgebra}
43	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Section 01LL ``Relative spectrum via glueing'',
44	  % Situation ``situation-relative-spec''
45	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L312-L318).
46	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
47	  % "Here \(S\) is a scheme, and \(\mathcal{A}\) is a quasi-coherent
48	  %  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-algebra. This means that \(\mathcal{A}\) is a
49	  %  sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-algebras which is quasi-coherent as an
50	  %  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module."
51	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LL (situation-relative-spec).}
52	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. A \emph{quasi-coherent sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebras} is a
53	  sheaf \(\mathcal{A}\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebras (i.e. an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module
54	  equipped with \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear multiplication and unit morphisms satisfying
55	  associativity, commutativity, and the unit law as sheaf morphisms) whose underlying
56	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module is quasi-coherent. We write \(\mathrm{QcohAlg}(X)\) for the
57	  category of quasi-coherent sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebras and
58	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebra morphisms.
59	\end{definition}
60	
61	% NOTE (iter-179 review): supersedes the iter-174 carrier note. The iter-179
62	% Block A refactor (analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md) upgrades the carrier
63	% to a 3-field structure
64	%     `structure QcohAlgebra (X : Scheme.{u}) where`
65	%     `  sheaf        : TopCat.Sheaf CommRingCat.{u} X.toPresheafedSpace`
66	%     `  unit         : X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf`
67	%     `  coequifibered : NatTrans.Coequifibered`
68	%     `    (Functor.whiskerLeft (AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor X).op unit.hom)`
69	% The new third field IS the iter-175+ overlay the prior note flagged: Mathlib
70	% commit `b80f227`'s Stacks-01LL `NatTrans.Coequifibered` predicate is strictly
71	% weaker than the full `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` claimed by Definition
72	% 1.1's prose but is equivalent for the geometric purpose (constructing the
73	% relative-spectrum scheme) via the dense-subsite equivalence
74	% `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`. This is the predicate Mathlib's
75	% `AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData` consumes, so the iter-179
76	% `RelativeSpec`/`structureMorphism` bodies (now real Mathlib values) close
77	% against it.
78	
79	\section{The relative-spectrum scheme}
80	\label{sec:relspec_scheme}

\chapter{Cross01 Substrate}
\label{chap:Genus0BaseObjects_Cross01Substrate}
% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/Cross01Substrate.lean

% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-189 plan-phase). This chapter is a project-bespoke
% substrate file landing two reusable lemmas needed to close the Lane B
% \(\mathbb{G}_m\)-scaling cocycle (path (III.c) of
% chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity) together with two off-target sorries
% (\texttt{gm\_geomIrred}, \texttt{projGm\_isReduced}) in
% \texttt{Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean}. Both substrates are
% generic in the geometric situation (the closed-immersion lift lemma is
% scheme-level; the tensor-domain lemma is ring-level keyed on the
% \texttt{projectiveLineBar} grading) and live in their own file rather
% than inline so they can be re-used by the three GmScaling
% applications and, if upstreamed, by Mathlib itself.
%
% The recipe consolidated below was produced by the
% \texttt{mathlib-analogist} subagent in iter-189 and is preserved
% verbatim in \texttt{analogies/lane-b-substrate.md} (Section
% ``Concrete project-side recipe''). The chapter itself is the
% prover-facing specification.

\section{Setup and motivation}
\label{sec:cross01_substrate_setup}

The Lane B cocycle \texttt{gmScalingP1\_chart\_agreement\_cross01} reduces, after
the iter-188 (III.c) separated-locus structural setup, to a single substantive
factorisation: a pair morphism
\(s_{\text{pair}} : T \to T_0 \times_X T_1\) into a fibre product over the
projective line bar \(\mathrm{PLB}_{\overline{k}}\) must factor through the
diagonal closed immersion
\(\Delta : T_0 \times_{X} T_1 \hookrightarrow T_0 \times_{\overline{k}} T_1\)
of the separated-over-\(\overline{k}\) target. Concretely the missing primitive is
the equivalence
\[
  \exists\, h : Y \to Z,\ h \circ i = g \iff \mathrm{range}(g) \subseteq \mathrm{range}(i)
\]
for a closed immersion \(i\) with reduced source/target hypothesis, which Mathlib
ships only in the kernel-inequality form (\(\mathtt{IsClosedImmersion.lift}\),
keyed on \(f.\mathrm{ker} \le g.\mathrm{ker}\)). Substrate~1 fills this gap.

The same Lane B cocycle, together with the two off-target instances
\texttt{gm\_geomIrred} and \texttt{projGm\_isReduced}, also requires
\[
  \mathrm{IsDomain}\bigl(\mathrm{Away}_{\overline{k}}\,f \otimes_{\overline{k}} \mathrm{GmRing}\,\overline{k}\bigr)
\]
for a homogeneous generator \(f\) of positive degree in the
\(\mathrm{projectiveLineBar}\) grading. Substrate~2 supplies this via a
canonical iso chain
\[
  \mathrm{Away}\,f \otimes_{\overline{k}} \overline{k}[t]
  \;\cong\; \mathrm{MvPolynomial}_{\mathrm{Unit}}(\mathrm{Away}\,f)
\]
followed by localisation at \(X()\), reducing the claim to the
already-formalised pattern from \texttt{projectiveLineBar\_isReduced}.

\section{Closed-immersion lift by range inclusion}
\label{sec:lift_iff_range_subset}

\begin{theorem}[lift through closed immersion via range containment]
  \label{thm:IsClosedImmersion_lift_iff_range_subset}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset}
  \uses{}
  Let \(X,Y,Z\) be schemes (Type \(u\)), let \(i : Z \to X\) be a closed
  immersion with \(Z\) reduced, and let \(g : Y \to X\) be a
  quasi-compact morphism with \(Y\) reduced. Then there exists a
  morphism \(h : Y \to Z\) with \(h \circ i = g\) if and only if the
  topological range of \(g\) is contained in the topological range of
  \(i\):
  \[
    (\exists\, h : Y \to Z,\ h \circ i = g)
    \;\iff\;
    \mathrm{range}(g_{\text{base}}) \subseteq \mathrm{range}(i_{\text{base}}).
  \]
\end{theorem}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{thm:IsClosedImmersion_lift_iff_range_subset}
  \emph{Forward direction} is range pushdown: if \(g = h \circ i\)
  then every point in \(\mathrm{range}(g_{\text{base}})\) is the image
  under \(i_{\text{base}}\) of \(h_{\text{base}}(y)\) for the
  corresponding preimage \(y\).

  \emph{Backward direction} threads the Galois connection between
  vanishing-ideal and support of an ideal sheaf. The proof has six
  steps; the Mathlib primitives cited are present at the project's
  pinned commit (\texttt{b80f227}).

  \begin{enumerate}
  \item \emph{Range of a closed immersion is its kernel support.}
    Because \(i\) is a closed immersion, \(i\) factors (up to
    isomorphism) as the canonical subscheme inclusion of its kernel
    ideal sheaf, so
    \(\mathrm{range}(i_{\text{base}}) = i.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support}\).
    (Mathlib:
    \texttt{IdealSheafData.range\_subschemeι} +
    \texttt{Scheme.Hom.toImage}.)
  \item \emph{Range of \(g\) lies in \(g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support}\).}
    The unconditional inclusion
    \(\mathrm{range}(g_{\text{base}}) \subseteq g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support}\)
    is shipped (\texttt{Scheme.Hom.range\_subset\_ker\_support}); under
    \([\mathrm{QuasiCompact}\;g]\), \(g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support}\)
    equals \(\overline{\mathrm{range}\,g_{\text{base}}}\)
    (\texttt{Scheme.Hom.support\_ker}). Since
    \(i.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support}\) is closed and contains
    \(\mathrm{range}(g)\) by hypothesis, the closure also lies in
    \(i.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support}\):
    \(g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support} \subseteq i.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support}\).
    (Mathlib: \texttt{closure\_minimal}.)
  \item \emph{\(Y\) reduced \(\Rightarrow\) \(g.\mathrm{ker}\) is radical.}
    Under \([\mathrm{QuasiCompact}\;g]\),
    \(g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{ideal}\,U = \mathrm{ker}(g.\mathrm{app}\,U)\)
    (\texttt{Scheme.Hom.ker\_apply}). Reducedness of \(Y\) makes this
    ring kernel a radical ideal pointwise on each affine open, so
    \(g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{radical} = g.\mathrm{ker}\).
  \item \emph{\(Z\) reduced \(\Rightarrow\) \(i.\mathrm{ker}\) is
    radical.} Identical argument with \(i\) in place of \(g\)
    (closed immersions are quasi-compact, so the
    \texttt{ker\_apply} hypothesis is satisfied automatically).
  \item \emph{Galois-connection chain.} Combining Steps~2--4 with
    \texttt{IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal\_support} (which equates
    the vanishing ideal of a support with the radical) gives
    \[
      i.\mathrm{ker}
        = i.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{radical}
        = \mathrm{vanishingIdeal}(i.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support})
        \le \mathrm{vanishingIdeal}(g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support})
        = g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{radical}
        = g.\mathrm{ker}.
    \]
    The middle inequality uses the antitone half of the Galois
    connection (\texttt{IdealSheafData.gc}).
  \item \emph{Apply Mathlib's kernel-inequality lift.} The proof
    concludes by feeding \(\mathrm{hker} : i.\mathrm{ker} \le g.\mathrm{ker}\)
    to \texttt{IsClosedImmersion.lift} and reading off its
    factorisation lemma \texttt{lift\_fac}.
  \end{enumerate}
\end{proof}

\section{Tensor reducedness over the algebraic closure}
\label{sec:tensor_AwayGm_isDomain}

\begin{theorem}[domain structure on \(\mathrm{Away}\,f \otimes_{\overline{k}} \mathrm{GmRing}\)]
  \label{thm:gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain}
  \uses{}
  Let \(\overline{k}\) be a field, let \(d \ge 1\), and let \(f\) be a
  non-zero homogeneous polynomial of degree \(d\) in the
  \(\mathrm{projectiveLineBar}\) grading on
  \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}(\mathrm{Fin}\,2)\;\overline{k}\). Then the
  tensor product
  \[
    \mathrm{HomogeneousLocalization.Away}\bigl(\mathrm{projectiveLineBarGrading}\,\overline{k}\bigr)\,f
    \;\otimes_{\overline{k}}\;
    \mathrm{GmRing}\;\overline{k}
  \]
  is a domain (and hence reduced).
\end{theorem}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{thm:gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain}
  The proof exhibits a canonical isomorphism of \(\overline{k}\)-algebras
  identifying the tensor product with the localisation of a
  polynomial ring over a domain at a non-zero-divisor; both operations
  preserve the domain property.

  \begin{enumerate}
  \item \emph{Domain instance on the homogeneous localisation.}
    \(\mathrm{Away}\,f := \mathrm{HomogeneousLocalization.Away}(\mathrm{projectiveLineBarGrading})\,f\)
    embeds via the \texttt{val\_injective} bridge into
    \(\mathrm{Localization.Away}\,f\); since the polynomial ring
    \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}(\mathrm{Fin}\,2)\;\overline{k}\) is a domain
    and \(f \ne 0\), the localisation is a domain, and the bridge
    transports the property to \(\mathrm{Away}\,f\). (Already used in
    \texttt{projectiveLineBar\_isReduced}; see
    \texttt{GmScaling.lean} L734--738 for the precise instance
    threading.)
  \item \emph{Collapse the tensor with the polynomial factor.}
    Recalling
    \(\mathrm{GmRing}\;\overline{k}
       = \mathrm{Localization.Away}(X()\,:\,\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\;\mathrm{Unit}\;\overline{k})\),
    apply \texttt{MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv} to get
    \[
      \mathrm{Away}\,f \otimes_{\overline{k}} \mathrm{MvPolynomial}\;\mathrm{Unit}\;\overline{k}
      \;\cong\;
      \mathrm{MvPolynomial}\;\mathrm{Unit}\;(\mathrm{Away}\,f),
    \]
    a \(\overline{k}\)-algebra (in fact \(\mathrm{Away}\,f\)-algebra)
    isomorphism.
  \item \emph{Push the localisation through the tensor.}
    Tensoring with the localisation step
    \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\;\mathrm{Unit}\;\overline{k} \to \mathrm{GmRing}\;\overline{k}\)
    factors through Mathlib's tensor-base-change lemma
    \texttt{IsLocalization.Away.tensorRightEquiv}, yielding an
    isomorphism
    \[
      \mathrm{Away}\,f \otimes_{\overline{k}} \mathrm{GmRing}\;\overline{k}
      \;\cong\;
      \mathrm{Localization.Away}(\iota(X())\,:\,\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\;\mathrm{Unit}\;(\mathrm{Away}\,f))
    \]
    where \(\iota\) is the canonical algebra map. The threading uses
    \texttt{IsScalarTower} compatibility and the
    \texttt{Algebra}/\(\mathrm{IsLocalization.Away}\) instance on the
    tensored polynomial ring.
  \item \emph{Right-hand side is a domain.}
    \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\;\mathrm{Unit}\;(\mathrm{Away}\,f)\) is a
    polynomial ring over a domain (Step~1), hence a domain by the
    standard instance. The generator \(X()\) is non-zero
    (\texttt{MvPolynomial.X\_ne\_zero}), so its powers form a
    submonoid of the non-zero-divisors
    (\texttt{powers\_le\_nonZeroDivisors\_of\_noZeroDivisors}); the
    localisation is then a domain by
    \texttt{IsLocalization.isDomain\_localization}.
  \end{enumerate}

  Transferring the domain property along the iso of Step~3 closes the
  goal. Reducedness is a direct corollary of being a domain.
\end{proof}

% NOTE (iter-189 plan-phase). The two substrates above are independent
% targets. Iter-189 prover dispatch lands Substrate~1
% (\(\sim 40\)--60 LOC). Substrate~2 is owed iter-190 prover dispatch
% (\(\sim 50\)--80 LOC). After both land, the three GmScaling.lean
% consumer sorries (\texttt{gmScalingP1\_chart\_agreement\_cross01},
% \texttt{gm\_geomIrred}, \texttt{projGm\_isReduced}) close in
% iters~191--193 via \(\sim 65\)--105 LOC of inline application
% (per the LOC table in \texttt{analogies/lane-b-substrate.md}).

% Out of scope:
% - The GmScaling.lean consumer applications (cocycle body, geometric
%   irreducibility instance, reducedness instance) are bodied in the
%   sibling chapter chap:Genus (and inline in GmScaling.lean once the
%   substrates are imported); they do NOT live here.
% - Upstreaming the lift_iff_range_subset variant to Mathlib is a
